2604.15841.title: Exploring the Capability Boundaries of LLMs in Mastering of Chinese Chouxiang Language
2604.15841.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in general language tasks, their performance on Chouxiang Language, a representative subcultural language in the Chinese internet context, remains largely unexplored. In this paper, we introduce Mouse, a specialized benchmark designed to evaluate the capabilities of LLMs on NLP tasks involving Chouxiang Language across six tasks. Experimental results show that, current state-of-the-art (SOTA) LLMs exhibit clear limitations on multiple tasks, while performing well on tasks that involve contextual semantic understanding. In addition, we further discuss the reasons behind the generally low performance of SOTA LLMs on Chouxiang Language, examine whether the LLM-as-a-judge approach adopted for translation tasks aligns with human judgments and values, and analyze the key factors that influence Chouxiang translation. Our study aims to promote further research in the NLP community on multicultural integration and the dynamics of evolving internet languages. Our code and data are publicly available.
2604.15841.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在一般語言任務中取得了顯著的成功，但它們在中國互聯網背景下的代表性亞文化語言——抽象語言上的表現仍然大多未被探索。在本文中，我們介紹了Mouse，一個專門設計的基準，用於評估LLMs在涉及抽象語言的六個NLP任務中的能力。實驗結果顯示，當前的最先進（SOTA）LLMs在多個任務上顯示出明顯的限制，而在涉及上下文語義理解的任務上表現良好。此外，我們進一步討論了SOTA LLMs在抽象語言上普遍低表現的原因，檢視了用於翻譯任務的LLM作為評判者的方法是否與人類的判斷和價值觀相符，並分析了影響抽象翻譯的關鍵因素。我們的研究旨在促進NLP社群對多元文化整合和不斷演變的互聯網語言動態的進一步研究。我們的代碼和數據已公開可用。
2604.15841.author: Dianqing Lin et.al.
2604.15841.authors: Dianqing Lin, Tian Lan, Jiali Zhu, Jiang Li, Wei Chen, Xu Liu, Aruukhan, Xiangdong Su, Hongxu Hou, Guanglai Gao
